     The Numbers Business Report

Spider-Man (2002)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $405,930,363Details
International Box Office $417,999,609
Worldwide Box Office $823,929,972
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Peter Parker who, after being bitten by a genetically-altered spider, gains superhuman strength and the spider-like ability to cling to any surface. He vows to use his abilities to fight crime, coming to understand the words of his beloved Uncle Ben: “With great power comes great responsibility.”

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$114,844,116 (28.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.53 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:49.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$139,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,615 opening theaters/3,876 max. theaters, 8.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $833,315,940

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 3rd, 2002 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
September 26th, 2025 (Special Engagement) by Fathom Events
International Releases: June 6th, 2002 (Wide) (Australia)
June 6th, 2002 (Wide) (New Zealand)
June 14th, 2002 (Wide), released as Örümcek-Adam (Turkey)
June 13th, 2024 (Special Engagement) (Netherlands)
August 2nd, 2024 (Special Engagement) (2024 Re-issue, one week only) (United Kingdom)
Video Release: November 1st, 2002 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
November 25th, 2002 by Sony Pictures
MPA Rating: PG-13 for stylized violence and action.
Running Time: 121 minutes
Franchise: Spider-Man
Cast, crew, or production detail: Visual Effects
Character Appearance: Spider-Man

Synopsis

Peter Parker who, after being bitten by a genetically-altered spider, gains superhuman strength and the spider-like ability to cling to any surface. He vows to use his abilities to fight crime, coming to understand the words of his beloved Uncle Ben: “With great power comes great responsibility.”
